related_queries: NovelCrafter bring your own key cost
must_cover_entities: Sudowrite (Muse 1.5, Story Bible, Story Engine, Canvas, Visualize, credits)
BYO models: OpenAI / Claude / local LLM
verified_facts: Sudowrite: own fiction-tuned model Muse 1.5; credit system; entry Hobby&Student ~$10/mo, paid tiers ~$19–$59/mo.
NovelCrafter: platform ~$4–$20/mo but AI usage billed separately via your own API key; Codex lore database; mobile; import/export Word/Markdown/HTML.
Philosophy: Sudowrite = guided creative partner ('Magic Wand'); NovelCrafter = configurable system ('Master Blueprint' / 'Photoshop of AI writing').
Sudowrite easier to start; NovelCrafter more control + privacy (data local) but steeper and uses general (non-fiction-tuned) models unless you pick one.
content_gaps: A real TRUE-COST worked example (Sudowrite credits vs NovelCrafter sub + API usage for an ~80k-word novel) — top results say 'cheaper on paper' but don't compute it.
A quick decision chooser ('pick X if you are Y') instead of a flat feature dump.
Honest prose-quality note: Muse 1.5 (fiction-tuned) vs NovelCrafter+BYO general model.
recommended_angle: An independent, no-vendor-bias chooser: philosophy contrast → side-by-side feature/pricing table → a worked true-cost example for an 80k novel → a 'pick this if you are…' decision section → FAQ. Lead with the BYO-key true-cost calc since that's the gap the SERP leaves open.
reason: Medium difficulty and clearly winnable: page one mixes vendor-biased posts (Sudowrite's own blog), a thin directory (Slashdot), and niche affiliate sites — an independent, genuinely useful chooser with a true-cost calculation can differentiate. Strong commercial intent + real affiliate fit for both tools, and it is a sensible authority-building entry point per the B1 deferral.
